title: Portfolio Case Studies and Technical Decision Narratives · Hiring Decision Lens: Failure Learning
summary: A project page celebrates features and tools but omits the user need, personal decisions, alternatives, failures, verification, accessibility, security, impact limits, and maintenance state. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the failure learning increment in evidence-linked project case-study set. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove.
Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Failure Learning · Theory Failure Learning
steps.instruction:
theory case — A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Failure Learning contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Failure Learning · Theory Failure Learning.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove. For Predict Failure Learning · Theory Failure Learning, keep Failure Learning connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Failure Learning condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct — Controls honesty about contribution and competence, privacy, confidentiality, fairness, harm avoidance, professional review, and accountability throughout a search.

options.text: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Portfolio material should hide failed approaches to appear competent. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Failure Learning.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

content.text: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. The worked example variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove. A defensible solution applies Failure Learning, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to evidence-linked project case-study set.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-2
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Portfolio material should hide failed approaches to appear competent. This fails the Failure Learning contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct (ACM Code 2018 current) — Controls honesty about contribution and competence, privacy, confidentiality, fairness, harm avoidance, professional review, and accountability throughout a search.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove.
